Diapedium moritzianum has been further classified into the genus Diapedium and the species D. moritzianum. intro: Diapedium moritzianum, also known as the "Ghanaian bush pepper," is a plant species belonging to the family Annonaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ghanaian medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Diapedium moritzianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
